title: The Making of ‘Oppenheimer’ with Hoyte Van Hoytema, Jennifer Lame, and Ludwig Göransson
format: Sean Fennessey talks to Oppenheimer's cinematographer, editor, and composer about shooting on IMAX film, cutting the Trinity test, and scoring quantum physics.

segments.heading: Making ‘Oppenheimer’
segments.guest: Hoyte Van Hoytema, Jennifer Lame & Ludwig Göransson on

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: Nolan had Kodak manufacture black-and-white 65mm film so the whole thing could be shot on IMAX and System 65, then printed on analog. Göransson built the hexatonic 'Can you hear the music' theme around a six-note scale and recorded the Trinity cue as one ten-minute live take; Lame cut the atom-collision montages before she was allowed to hear any of the score.

segments.heading: The last great thing they've seen

picks.title: All of Us Strangers
picks.note: "Made me feel a lot of feelings."

picks.title: Anatomy of a Fall
picks.note: Liked that it's about what's even true. "People talking in rooms as well."
